summary: ContextPacket Service has zero external consumers — likely dead code
details: {"severity":"HIGH","finding":"The ContextPacket Service (Packet.Service, Packet.use, Packet.layer) has zero external consumers. assembleL1, assembleL2, getCurrentPacket are only called internally. No file imports or yields Packet.Service. No layer composition references Packet.layer. The service appears to be dead code or intended for future wiring. This means: (1) any changes to the interfaces have zero downstream breakage risk, and (2) the claims separation work can proceed without worrying about consumer compatibility.","evidence":["grep for Packet.use|Packet.Service|Packet.layer across entire packages/opencode → 0 matches","grep for assembleL1|assembleL2|getCurrentPacket outside packet.ts → 0 matches","grep for @opencode/ContextPacket service tag outside packet.ts → 0 matches","barrel export at context/index.ts:7 is the only external reference to the packet module"]}

summary: claims.owned mixes coordination reservations with event-derived file paths
details: {"severity":"HIGH","finding":"In assembleL1, claims.owned is built from two incompatible sources: (1) Coordination.getSessionReservations(sessionId) which returns PathReservation[] from the coordination ledger (real claim data), and (2) extractFilePaths(recentEvents) as a fallback, which extracts filePath from runtime events. Event-derived file paths are NOT ownership claims — they are merely files the agent referenced. The two sources are conflated into a single claims.owned array at line 222-225. This conflates two distinct semantic categories: real ownership claims from the coordination ledger vs. activity-derived file references.","fix_direction":"Separate the event-derived paths into a distinct field (e.g., workingSet.recentFiles or a new recentFiles field in either ContextPacketL1 or ContextPacketL2). Keep claims.owned as coordination-ledger-only data. The conflictFiles derivation (ownedFiles ∩ dirtyFiles) should then use ONLY the coordination-derived owned files, not the event-derived paths."}

summary: Duplicate extractFilePaths function in packet.ts and tools.ts
details: {"severity":"MINOR","finding":"Two near-identical extractFilePaths functions exist: packet.ts:119-129 (takes RuntimeEventType[]) and tools.ts:418-444 (takes readonly { readonly filePath?: string }[]). Both extract unique filePath values from events. Consider extracting a shared utility.","duplicate_location":"packages/opencode/src/context/tools.ts:418-444"}
